mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative returns with accuracy above 55% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 285 index level triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with >55% accuracy over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.8 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >60% of months where housing starts drop below the threshold.
